description: Repertoire of dynastic-fall / loyalist (遺民) allusion in classical Chinese poetry, graded by loyalist specificity. specificity 1.0 = the trope almost exclusively signals dynastic loss or loyalist refusal; 0.5 = strongly associated but multivalent in general verse; 0.3 = associated but common in ordinary use. 'forms' lists surface substrings used for matching (traditional-character orthography). Each entry is anchored to its classical locus.
grading_note: Specificity weights are the analyst's prior and are reported openly; the primary analysis uses the high-specificity subset (>=0.8) so conclusions do not hinge on the multivalent tail. A sensitivity run reweights all entries to 1.0.

categories.dynastic_fall: mourning a fallen realm / ruined capital
categories.loyalty_refusal: refusing to serve the new master; loyalist martyrdom
categories.exile_displacement: the loyal minister exiled; the gentry's southward flight
categories.reclusion: withdrawal from a fallen world as political refusal
categories.ming_zheng_taiwan: the local Ming-loyalist / Koxinga-Dongning layer specific to Taiwan
categories.direct_diction: explicit loyalist lyric vocabulary (not a named historical allusion) — the register 1895 poets used directly

entries.gloss_en: 'millet in rows' over the ruined Zhou capital
entries.note: The canonical trope for mourning a fallen dynasty's capital.

entries.gloss_en: Jizi's lament over the ruins of Yin
entries.locus: 史記·宋微子世家 (箕子 麥秀歌)
entries.note: Paired with 黍離 as the archetypal dynastic-ruin lament.

entries.gloss_en: bronze camels lying in brambles — a capital gone to ruin
entries.note: Suo Jing foreseeing the fall of Luoyang.

entries.gloss_en: the sinking of the sacred land (China lost)
entries.note: Highly resonant for 1895: the realm itself founders.

entries.gloss_en: the Jingkang catastrophe (Northern Song's fall, 1127)
entries.note: A named historical dynastic disaster invoked as analogy.

entries.gloss_en: Yashan — the last Song stand and mass drowning, 1279
entries.note: The end-of-dynasty martyr-sea; strongly evoked at any final fall.

entries.gloss_en: the city where captured Song emperors were held
entries.note: Captivity of a fallen royal house.

entries.gloss_en: the bronze immortal weeping as it is carted from fallen Han
entries.note: Object-mourning of a dynasty's passing.

entries.gloss_en: the old realm / former capital
entries.note: Multivalent; loyalist when paired with ruin imagery.

entries.gloss_en: the realm shattered though mountains and rivers remain
entries.locus: 杜甫·春望; 文天祥·過零丁洋
entries.note: Du Fu / Wen Tianxiang fusion trope.

entries.gloss_en: the moving of the tripods — change of dynasty
entries.locus: 左傳 (problem of the nine tripods)
entries.note: Euphemism for dynastic transfer.

entries.gloss_en: change of dynasty / generation
rise and fall (of states)
entries.note: Common; loyalist only in context.

entries.gloss_en: mulberry-fields-to-sea — drastic worldly upheaval
entries.note: General vicissitude; loyalist in dynastic context.

entries.gloss_en: the deposed Shu king reborn as a cuckoo weeping blood
entries.locus: 華陽國志; 蜀王本紀
entries.note: A dethroned ruler's unending grief.

entries.gloss_en: Bo Yi & Shu Qi starving rather than eat Zhou grain
entries.note: Supreme emblem of refusing to serve the conqueror.

entries.gloss_en: Bo Yi and Shu Qi
entries.note: Named loyalist exemplars.

entries.gloss_en: Tian Heng and his five hundred who died rather than submit
entries.note: Collective suicide over surrender.

entries.gloss_en: the captive of Chu keeping his homeland's cap
entries.note: Loyalty preserved in captivity.

entries.gloss_en: Wen Tianxiang, Song loyalist martyr; 'leave a loyal heart to shine in the annals'
entries.locus: 文天祥·過零丁洋, 正氣歌
entries.note: The paradigmatic loyalist-martyr citation.

entries.gloss_en: Zhang Xun & Xu Yuan's doomed defense of Suiyang
entries.note: Loyalist defense to the death.

entries.gloss_en: Su Wu holding the Han tally through 19 years' captivity
entries.note: Unbroken loyalty in exile.

entries.gloss_en: Lu Zhonglian, who would drown rather than serve Qin
entries.note: Refusal of an illegitimate sovereign.

entries.gloss_en: Shen Baoxu weeping seven days at the Qin court to restore Chu
entries.note: Loyalty seeking restoration of the lost state.

entries.gloss_en: Yan Gaoqing cursing the rebel with his tongue cut out
entries.note: In Wen Tianxiang's roll-call of loyalists.

entries.gloss_en: Zu Ti's mid-river vow to recover the lost north
entries.note: Resolve to reconquer the homeland.

entries.gloss_en: the Jingwei bird filling the sea — futile, unyielding resistance
entries.note: Hopeless but unsurrendering effort.

entries.gloss_en: Qu Yuan, the loyal minister exiled and drowned
entries.locus: 史記·屈原列傳; 楚辭
entries.note: Loyal grief at a ruler's rejection; suicide in the river.

entries.gloss_en: Yu Xin's 'Lament for the South' in forced northern exile
entries.note: Mourning a fallen southern court from captivity.

entries.gloss_en: Wang Can climbing the tower, longing for a homeland in chaos
entries.note: Exile and homesickness amid dynastic disorder.

entries.gloss_en: the Jin exiles weeping at Xinting — 'the scenery is unchanged, the realm is not'
entries.note: The exact emotional posture of conquered gentry.

entries.gloss_en: the gentry's southward crossing after the north fell
entries.note: Mass displacement of a conquered elite.

entries.gloss_en: the homeland gate / native garden
entries.note: General homesickness; low specificity.

entries.gloss_en: Peach Blossom Spring — refuge from a tyrannous age (避秦)
entries.note: 避秦 raises specificity: fleeing conquest.

entries.gloss_en: Tao Qian's withdrawal from office
entries.note: Reclusion; loyalist only in pointed context.

entries.gloss_en: the Four Hoaryheads who withdrew from a usurped court
entries.note: Recluse-loyalists.

entries.gloss_en: Xu You washing his ears of the offer of empire
entries.note: Refusal of political office as purity.

entries.gloss_en: the fisherman of Canglang — withdrawal from a muddy world
entries.note: Tied to the Qu Yuan dialogue.

entries.gloss_en: Yan Guang refusing to serve Emperor Guangwu
entries.note: Principled refusal of a new reign.

entries.gloss_en: the 'leftover subject' of a fallen dynasty
entries.note: The title concept; explicit loyalist self-naming.

entries.gloss_en: the isolated loyal minister
entries.note: Self-image of the powerless loyalist.

entries.gloss_en: the former sovereign / prior dynasty
the rivers-and-mountains (the realm/territory)
entries.note: Multivalent; loyalist when tied to loss/cession.

entries.gloss_en: the ceding/abandonment of territory (incl. of Taiwan); the Maguan treaty
entries.note: Direct reference to the 1895 cession; high salience, some general use.

entries.gloss_en: to reverse heaven's course / mend the sky — to save a doomed realm
entries.note: The impotent wish to undo the fall.

entries.gloss_en: Fan Li retiring by skiff after serving — honourable withdrawal
entries.note: Withdrawal after the state's fate is sealed.

entries.gloss_en: to swallow grief / harbour lasting regret
entries.note: Low specificity; grief register.

entries.gloss_en: Koxinga / Prince of Yanping, Ming-loyalist founder of Taiwan resistance
entries.locus: 臺灣外記; 臺灣通史
entries.note: The local Ming-loyalist hero; doubly charged after 1895.

entries.gloss_en: Dongning — the Zheng regime's name for Ming-loyalist Taiwan
entries.note: Names Taiwan as a loyalist refuge-polity.

entries.gloss_en: Prince Ningjing, the Ming prince who died in Taiwan
entries.note: Royal Ming martyrdom on Taiwan soil.

entries.gloss_en: the Five Consorts who died with Prince Ningjing
entries.note: A local loyalist-martyr shrine, frequent poem subject.

entries.gloss_en: Shen Guangwen, founding Ming-loyalist poet of Taiwan
entries.note: Literary ancestor of Taiwan's loyalist verse.

entries.gloss_en: the (lost) house of Ming
entries.note: 思明 (Xiamen) literally 'longing for Ming'.
